title: Slendor Magnetic Stirrer SH-2 Hot Plate Mixer Max 520℉ Lab Hotplate Stirrer 2000 RPM Stir Plate with Thermometer, Stirrer Bar and Support Stand
description:
About this item
Adjustable Stirring Speed -- There is a speed regulating device at the bottom of the SH-2 magnetic stirrer hot plate. Rotate clockwise to increase the stirring speed, and counterclockwise to decrease. Adjust as needed. The max stirring speed is up to 2000RPM.
Rapid Heating -- Heating Power: 180W, Max Temp: 520°F. The magnetic hotplate stirre can stirrer and heat at the same time. Included 1 inch Teflon coated magnetic stirrer bar and thermometer, which can be used to display the temperature during use.
Advanced Material -- The magnetic stir plate is made of advanced materials, resistant to high temperature, abrasion, and chemical corrosion. Each stir hot plate stirrer has been tested and inspected, and the quality is guaranteed.
Widely Used -- The magnetic heated plate is suitable for scientific research institutions, industry, agriculture, laboratories and universities. Tips: work continuously for 4 hours in high-speed state and 8 hours in medium-speed state.
Quality Assurance -- The professional magnetic hotplate stirrer has undergone strict quality inspection and can operate stably for long time. If you have any questions about the lab hot plate, please contact the seller customer service at any time.
reviews_5s:
Did I ever like this hot plate? No. I'm used to working on Corning units in professional labs that have chemical resistance, precise temp control, 550°C max temp, powerful incremental stirring, and LCD display.

This unit has none of these things, and is quite frustrating to use.

To start, the knobs mean basically nothing. The heating element is only slightly warm until you crank it to max at which point its ripping hot. The stirring is only slightly more incremental, and quite shockingly weak as hot plates go. It also developed an issue early on where I had to fiddle with the knobs for like 3 minutes to get it to actually start heating at all.
The hot plate surface has very minimal chemical resistance, and was permanently discolored by 2M HCl. It also happens to be designed in a way that's very difficult to clean/maintenance.
AND all 4 little rubber feet melted from the heat of the hot plate itself within a month.

Despite all that, this little unit lasted 7 whole months of very constant hobby use, which exceeded my initial expectations. It probably could have lasted longer but a beaker of boiling piranha solution spilled all over it.. which finally killed it for good.

That said, I became quite fond of this little thing over the months along with all its annoying quirks, and I admit I'll miss it. At the end of the day the low cost helps with the barrier of entry into hobby chemistry, and it always (eventually) got the job done. I highly recommend this unit if you want to get into hobby chemistry, but aren't sure how long the hobby is going to last.Gets very hot and temperature setting is helpful, requires careful useWorks great! Does what it's meant to do.I like itSo I bargain shopped for some stuff to teach my kids some chemistry at home and wound up with this little 180w hot plate and a comically large ehrlenmeyer flask… ended up running this full blast with stirring for 72hrs straight to simmer something down and it worked great!This is a perfect budget stirrer hotplate, it will get the job done and done and done and done. I've put mine through its paces, and it seems no worse for wear! I would advise plugging this into a cheap wattage meter plug if you care about repeatability, though. Reading the input wattage can be MUCH more reproduceable than relying on the dial, especially in lower temperature ranges. The amount of dial-turn from 0 watts to 90 watts is quite small, and the give on the dial makes consistency difficult. That said, if you need a temperature controlled hotplate, this isn't that - so a bit of a moot point! It does what it's supposed to, very well, for a good price.This is a great company who went above & beyond to make their customers happy. My first order had the little magnetic stirrers missing, they were on it to make it more then right & I am very happy with the product.I need a heated mixer for some fragrance and wax experiments for my candle company. This one is simple and works great. I've been using it for several weeks, saving me so much time. It heats up quickly and holds the temperature well. I also love that it includes all the necessary components, like the stirring rod and stand.Envío súper rápido a Cali ColombiaHasta el momento trabaja muy bien, con ayuda del termómetro se puede controlar la temperatura, es muy pequeña, pero es buena.
reviews_1s: After I turned the machine on for the first time, it only worked for several seconds, and it stopped. The light is off and can never be turned on again. I saw similar reviews. Please re-evaluate the design and the functionality of the wires or electric board. I was in the middle of preparing a reagent for a lab and had to manually stir the solution, ending up wasted quite some time.temperature control didn't work it was no power or full power no control what soever. spend the extra money and buy a ONILAB. If you listen to this review will save you time and moneyI had several people recommend this device. When it arrived, I didn't need to use it immediately. I wish I had unpacked it and tested it anyway. Today I unpacked it to use and discovered that it will not even power on. I missed my return window by a week! ...Money wasted...no means to contact seller for resolutionDo not buy this plate if you need to maintain a temp below 120° F. Very disappointed in low quality thermostat controlOnly reached a liquid temperature of 208F with water on the highest setting, save your money and look elsewhere cause this is not accurate at all.Product advertises 2000rpm but manual states max rpm is 1600The plate itself is not flat.I plugged in the Slendor SH-2 Magnetic stirrer/hot plate with all switches in the off position. It blew the GFI outlet everytime immediately upon plugging it in, without even turning it on. Then I go to a heavy amp rated outlet and plug it in. No breaker tripped and the lights go on when I turn on the switches. Thought I was good, but then I noticed the heat plate was hot cuz I put my hand on it while making some adjustments. The hot plate was turned off but it still heated up. I had to unplug the stirrer/hot plate to shut down the hot plate. Obviously a huge safety issue and I got minor burns only cuz I have quick reflexes. I almost ran on an errand after I turned the hot plate off, thinking it would cool down. It kept getting hotter. Could of burned down my house and my pets. DONT TRUST IT.The stirrer doesn’t work as expected. It fails to maintain the correct temperature and shuts off by itself multiple times. Very disappointing!
